meaning: adj.秘密的,偷偷摸摸的,[生理]分泌的,促进分沁的
examples: While the terror group established a large, secretive external operations wing bent on attacking the West, it has also cultivated a backup strategy of enabling and inspiring attacks by individuals.
He's very secretive about his work.
Billionaires are usually fairly secretive about the exact amount that they're worth.
He has broken with the tradition of the secretive committee, whose members rarely discuss proceedings.
Milk and Honey, a secretive bar on the Lower East Side, recently tightened its admission policy.
